title: Central London Congestion Charging: Impacts Monitoring Sixth Annual Report
text: Congestion charging reduced traffic entering the charging zone by approximately 21% relative to pre-charge levels, with revenue reinvested into bus services.

title: Congestion Pricing in Practice: A Comparative Review
text: Across cordon pricing schemes, peak vehicle entries fell 15-20%, while distributional effects depended heavily on exemption design and transit reinvestment.

title: MBTA Ridership and Service Open Data Export
text: Downtown Boston transit corridors retain spare peak capacity on several rapid-transit lines, indicating room to absorb mode shift from any pricing program.

title: Central Business District Tolling: Equity Considerations
text: Low-income drivers without transit alternatives bear a higher relative burden; targeted exemptions and credits materially reduce regressivity.
